title.en: PULP COMPOSITIONS; PREPARATION THEREOF NOT COVERED BY SUBCLASSES D21C OR D21D; IMPREGNATING OR COATING OF PAPER; TREATMENT OF FINISHED PAPER NOT COVERED BY CLASS B31 OR SUBCLASS D21G; PAPER NOT OTHERWISE PROVIDED FOR
{Paper; Cardboard (fibreboard D21J)}
{Multi-ply material finished plies}

{Paper or cardboard prepared by adding substances to the pulp or to the formed web on the paper-making machine and by applying substances to finished paper or cardboard (on the paper-making machine), also when the intention is to impregnate at least a part of the paper body}

{by adding insoluble coloured substances, e.g. powders, fibres, pieces of metal, for obtaining different colours in the paper fancy papers; substances characterised by their physical appearance, e.g. form, rather than by their chemical constitution}
{Special paper or cardboard not otherwise provided for (duplicating or recording paper B41M)}
{Flame-resistant papers; (complex) compositions rendering paper fire-resistant}

{Processes or apparatus specially adapted for applying liquids or other fluent materials to finished paper or board, e.g. impregnating, coating (applying liquids to surfaces in general B05; treating textile materials by liquids, gases or vapours D06B; impregnated or coated fibreboard D21J1/08; apparatus for making patterned paper D21H5/06; printing machines B41F)}
{Anti-slip papers}

{Anti-friction, anti-abrasive or release paper (processes for obtaining an anti-friction or anti-adhesive surface B05D5/08; adhesive materials on paper characterised by the release coating composition C09J7/21)}
{Transparent papers, e.g. paper treated with transparent-rendering compositions or glassine paper prepared from well-hydrated paper stock}
{characterised by the use of special fibrous materials as well as special compounds (use of special fibrous materials D21H5/12; adding substances to the pulp or to the formed web D21H3/00)}
{Wall papers (printed wallpapers B41M3/18; pregummed wall paper C09J7/21)}
{Paper for surface-protection and decorative purposes, e.g. pressure laminates (wall paper D21H5/0082; multi-ply material D21H1/02)}

{Post-treated paper (after-treatment following application of substances to finished paper D21H25/00 - D21H25/18, D21H5/0062; after-treatment of printed works B41M7/00; working paper B31F; paper from fibres which can be modified D21H5/1272)}
{Patterned paper}
{Vegetable parchment}
{characterised by the use of special fibrous materials (felts or other non-woven fabrics D04)}
{Fungicidal, bactericidal, insecticidal, disinfecting, antiseptic, or corrosion-inhibiting paper antistatic, antioxygenic paper (toilet paper A47K10/16)}
{having enhanced flexibility or extensibility produced by mechanical treatment of the unfinished paper (crêping paper B31F1/12; making patterned paper D21F11/006, apertured paper D21F11/008)}

{Special paper or cardboard manufactured by dry method; Apparatus or processes for forming webs by dry method from mainly short-fibre or particle material, e.g. paper pulp (making board from wood, e.g. lignocellulosic, particles or fibres B27N1/00 and subgroups; making non-woven fabrics from textile fibres D04H1/72; machines for forming diapers A61F13/15585; lap-forming devices in preliminary treatment of fibres, e.g. for spinning D01G25/00)}
Pulp or paper, comprising cellulose or lignocellulose fibres of natural origin only
Chemical or chemomechanical {or chemothermomechanical} pulp
Mechanical or thermomechanical pulp
Mixtures of chemical and mechanical pulp
Pulp from non-woody plants or crops, e.g. cotton, flax, straw, bagasse
Secondary fibres (working-up waste paper D21C5/02)
modified by a particular after-treatment
Pulp or paper, comprising synthetic cellulose or non-cellulose fibres or web-forming material (chemical features in the manufacture of artificial fibres D01F)
Synthetic cellulose fibres
Organic non-cellulose fibres
Inorganic fibres or flakes
Pulp or paper, comprising fibres or web-forming material characterised by features other than their chemical constitution
characterised by configuration
Non-fibrous material added to the pulp, characterised by its constitution; Paper-impregnating material characterised by its constitution
{Microorganisms or enzymes}
Waste products, e.g. sludge
Material of vegetable origin (proteins D21H17/22; lignins D21H17/23; polysaccharides D21H17/24; rosin D21H17/62)
Non-macromolecular organic compounds
Macromolecular organic compounds
Waxes
Bitumen
Rosin; Derivatives thereof
Inorganic compounds
{Mixtures of material (D21H17/69 takes precedence); Pulp or paper comprising several different materials not incorporated by special processes (D21H23/10, D21H23/70, D21H23/76 take precedence)}
Coated paper (coated fibreboard D21J1/08); Coating material (recording sheets characterised by the coating used to improve ink, dye or pigment receptivity B41M5/50)
Metal coatings (D21H19/66 takes precedence)
Coatings without pigments (D21H19/66 takes precedence)
Coatings with pigments (D21H19/66 takes precedence; metal powder D21H19/06)
Coatings characterised by a special visual effect, e.g. patterned, textured (marbled paper D21H27/04)
Coated paper characterised by the paper substrate
Paper comprising more than one coating (D21H19/02 takes precedence)
Non-fibrous material added to the pulp, characterised by its function, form or properties; Paper-impregnating or coating material, characterised by its function, form or properties
Agents for preventing deposition on the paper mill equipment, e.g. pitch or slime control (removal of fats, resins, pitch, or waxes D21C9/08)
Paper forming aids
characterised by function or properties in or on the paper (D21H19/66, D21H27/02 take precedence)
characterised by form (D21H19/66, D21H21/42, D21H27/02 take precedence)

Processes or apparatus for adding material to the pulp or to the paper (applying liquids or other fluent material to surfaces, in general B05; processes for making continuous lengths of paper D21F11/00)
characterised by the manner in which substances are added

characterised by choice of auxiliary compounds which are added separately from at least one other compound, e.g. to improve the incorporation of the latter or to obtain an enhanced combined effect (D21H17/18, D21H17/70, D21H23/10 take precedence)
Controlling or regulating not limited to any particular process or apparatus
After-treatment of paper not provided for in groups D21H17/00 - D21H23/00
{Mechanical treatment (D21H25/08, D21H25/18 take precedence)}
Chemical or biochemical treatment (D21H25/18 takes precedence)
Physical treatment, e.g. heating, irradiating (D21H25/18 takes precedence; dryer section of machines for making continuous webs of paper D21F5/00)
Rearranging applied substances, e.g. metering, smoothing; Removing excess material
of old paper as in books, documents, e.g. restoring
Special paper not otherwise provided for, e.g. made by multi-step processes
{Release paper}
{Tissue paper; Absorbent paper (D21H21/22, D21H27/02, D21H27/20 take precedence; toilet paper A47K10/00; absorbent pads for physiological fluids A61L15/16; making on paper-making machines D21F11/00)}
Patterned paper (patterned coatings D21H19/66; embossing B31F1/07; prepared on the paper-making machines D21F11/00)
Vegetable or imitation parchment; Glassine paper
Filter paper (self-supporting filtering material B01D39/14; making on paper-making machines D21F11/14)
Packing paper (packaging materials of special type or form B65D65/38)
Paper having stable form or dimension; Curl-resistant paper (anticoil photographic support G03C1/81)
Pure paper, i.e. paper lacking or having low content of contaminants (after-treatment of cellulose pulp D21C9/00)
Paper- or board-based structures for surface covering
Multi-ply (for surface covering D21H27/18; making on paper-making machines D21F9/00, D21F11/00)
